content.cn_text: 现在请翻到第四部分。
content.en_text: Now, turn to Section 4.

content.cn_text: 第四部分。你将听到一位发展研究讲师
content.en_text: Section 4 You will hear a lecturer in development studies

content.cn_text: 讲解季节性迁牧。
content.en_text: talking about transhumance.

content.cn_text: 这是指人群带着他们的动物从一个地方迁移到另一个地方的方式。
content.en_text: That is the way groups of people move from place to place with their animals.

content.cn_text: 首先，你有一些时间查看第31至第40题。
content.en_text: First, you have some time to look at questions 31 to 40.

content.cn_text: 现在请仔细听，并回答第31至第40题。
content.en_text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 31 to 40.

content.cn_text: 大家下午好。
content.en_text: Good afternoon, everyone.

content.cn_text: 今天我要讲一个对你们来说可能比较新的概念，也就是人们带着他们的
content.en_text: Today, I'm going to talk about what may be a new concept to you, the movement of people with their

content.cn_text: 畜群从一个地方迁移到另一个地方。
content.en_text: herds of animals from place to place.

content.cn_text: 这被称为季节性迁牧。
content.en_text: This is known as transhumance.

content.cn_text: 这些人是牧民，他们的动物包括牛、羊、山羊、马、
content.en_text: These people are pastoralists and their animals include cows, sheep, goats, horses,

content.cn_text: 骆驼和驯鹿。
content.en_text: camels and reindeer.

content.cn_text: 现在，研究人员有时会区分两种季节性迁牧：固定型和游牧型，
content.en_text: Now, researchers sometimes make a distinction between two types of transhumance, fixed and nomadic,

content.cn_text: 并讨论区分二者的各种因素。
content.en_text: and talk about various factors which differentiate between them.

content.cn_text: 他们做出的第一个也是最重要的区分与气候有关。
content.en_text: The first and most important distinction they make is to do with the climate.

content.cn_text: 固定型迁牧出现在瑞士等国家，在这些地方季节在天气和时间方面
content.en_text: Fixed transhumance occurs in countries such as Switzerland, where seasons are predictable

content.cn_text: 都是可预测的。
content.en_text: in terms of weather and timing.

content.cn_text: 而另一方面，游牧型迁牧通常出现在气候
content.en_text: And nomadic transhumance, on the other hand, is generally practiced in areas where climatic

content.cn_text: 条件难以预测的地区。
content.en_text: conditions are difficult to predict.

content.cn_text: 索马里就是这样的一个例子，因为预期中的降雨常常不会出现。
content.en_text: An example of such a location is Somalia, as the anticipated rains often just don't appear.

content.cn_text: 其次，研究人员有时会考察牧民所进行的旅程类型，也就是他们的移动方式。
content.en_text: Secondly, researchers sometimes look at the type of journey that pastoralists make, their movement.

content.cn_text: 对于固定型迁牧，这种移动模式有时被称为垂直型，因为它通常包括
content.en_text: For fixed transhumance, the pattern is sometimes known as vertical, because it typically consists

content.cn_text: 冬季从低地迁往高地，夏季再反向迁移。
content.en_text: of a journey from lower ground in the winter season to higher ground in the summer and vice versa.

content.cn_text: 当然，在野外，动物也经常自行遵循这类迁徙模式。
content.en_text: In the wild, of course, animals frequently follow these kinds of migration patterns themselves.

content.cn_text: 游牧型迁牧通常发生在更长距离上，而且复杂得多。
content.en_text: Nomadic transhumance generally takes place over a longer distance, and it's far more complex.

content.cn_text: 实际模式每年都不同，主要由动物对草和水的需求决定。
content.en_text: The actual pattern varies from year to year, and it's determined primarily by the animal's needs for grass and also for water.

content.cn_text: 另一个区分有时是根据住所来做的。
content.en_text: Another distinction is sometimes made in terms of homes.

content.cn_text: 对于固定型迁牧，这一群体通常在山谷中有永久住所，并在山上有一个
content.en_text: In the case of fixed transhumance, the group will typically have permanent homes in the valley and a shelter,

content.cn_text: 半永久性庇护所，比如小木屋，用来提供一定的天气防护。
content.en_text: which is semi-permanent in the mountains, such as a cabin, to offer some protection from the weather.

content.cn_text: 然而，在游牧型迁牧中，牧民总是住在临时庇护所中，
content.en_text: In nomadic transhumance, however, the pastoralists always live in temporary shelters,

content.cn_text: 这些住所由他们的牲畜从一个地方运到另一个地方，通常轻便可携。
content.en_text: which are transported by their livestock from place to place, and are usually light and portable.

content.cn_text: 例如，在肯尼亚北部，Gabra 牧民会把住所框架放在骆驼身上运输，
content.en_text: In Northern Kenya, for example, the Gabra pastoralist carries the frame of his home on his camel,

content.cn_text: 晚上停下来时再用树枝覆盖它。
content.en_text: and then covers it with branches when he stops for the night.

content.cn_text: 第四个常被提到的因素是参与人数。
content.en_text: A fourth factor often mentioned is the number of people taking part.

content.cn_text: 对于固定型迁牧，只有真正照看畜群的人会迁移。
content.en_text: In the case of fixed transhumance, only the people who actually look after the herds travel.

content.cn_text: 这些人通常是年轻人，因为他们更容易承受不利的身体条件。
content.en_text: These are normally the young ones because they can withstand adverse physical conditions more easily.

content.cn_text: 相比之下，在游牧型迁牧中，整个群体都会跟随牲畜迁移。
content.en_text: By contrast, in nomadic transhumance, the whole group moves with their livestock.

content.cn_text: 研究人员经常比较的最后一点是饮食和物资。
content.en_text: The last points of comparison often made by researchers are diet and goods.

content.cn_text: 在固定型迁牧中，那些不离开山谷的人常常开始做农民，耕种土地。
content.en_text: In fixed transhumance, those who do not move from the valleys often start working as farmers, cultivating the land.

content.cn_text: 他们与其他社区建立联系，有时也会用动物交换谷物。
content.en_text: They build up contacts with other communities, and in some cases, they may also trade animals for grain.

content.cn_text: 在游牧型迁牧中，与外界联系较少。
content.en_text: In nomadic transhumance, there are fewer contacts.

content.cn_text: 这个群体通常被认为能满足自己的全部需求，
content.en_text: The group are generally regarded as providers of all their own needs,

content.cn_text: 他们从畜群中获得奶酪和肉类等食物，也获得衣物。
content.en_text: and they obtain food such as cheese and meat, and also their clothing from their herds.

content.cn_text: 另一个例子可以在北非的阿尔及利亚找到，用来看看这个模型在当地迁牧中适用到什么程度。
content.en_text: Another example is found in Algeria in North Africa, to see how far this model applies to transhumance there.

content.cn_text: 从山谷到山地牧场的固定型迁牧在该地区很常见，
content.en_text: Fixed transhumance from valley to mountain pastures is common in the area,

content.cn_text: 但一些近来的牧民也会在春末带着畜群进行更长距离的迁移，
content.en_text: but some recent pastoralists also take their herds on longer journeys at the end of spring,

content.cn_text: 从南部的撒哈拉迁往北部平原，那里降雨更加充足。
content.en_text: from the Sahara in the south to the plains in the north, where there is more abundant rainfall.

content.cn_text: 传统上，他们会在临时居住当地期间帮助当地农民收割。
content.en_text: They have traditionally assisted local farmers with the harvest, which takes place while they are temporarily resident there.

content.cn_text: 我们需要判断他们的迁移属于固定型还是游牧型迁牧。
content.en_text: We have to decide whether their move would fall within the category of fixed or nomadic transhumance.

content.cn_text: 确实，他们住在帐篷里，这些帐篷可移动，而且全年都是临时住所，无论他们是在撒哈拉还是在阿尔及利亚北部。
content.en_text: It is true they live in tents, and these are movable, and temporary right through the year, whether they are in the Sahara or in the north of Algeria.

content.cn_text: 所以，从住所来看，他们似乎是游牧型。
content.en_text: So, in considering their homes, they appear nomadic.

content.cn_text: 另一方面，从移动模式来看，他们并不是游牧型。
content.en_text: On the other hand, in relation to their patterns of movement, they do not.

content.cn_text: 他们每年都赶着牲畜穿过相同的山口和山区，这表明他们属于固定型迁牧。
content.en_text: They drive their livestock through the same passes and mountains each year, which suggests fixed transhumance.

content.cn_text: 事实上，这里是一个显示两种迁牧类型特征的案例，我们可以更准确地称之为中间型。
content.en_text: Here, in fact, we have a case showing some features of both types of transhumance, which we might more precisely refer to as intermediate.

content.cn_text: 这一切都说明，你应该非常谨慎地对待每一种理论，如果案例不符合模型，就不要强行套入。
content.en_text: This all goes to show that you should treat every theory with great care, and not force your case history into a model if it doesn't fit.

content.cn_text: 第四部分到此结束。现在你有半分钟检查答案。
content.en_text: That is the end of section 4. You now have half a minute to check your answers.

groups.desc:
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
groups.title: Questions 31–40

items.title: for fixed transhumance, the pattern of movement is described as 31. __________
items.analysis: 答案解析：第31题问固定型迁牧的移动模式被描述为什么。原文说 the pattern is sometimes known as vertical，所以答案是 Vertical。

items.title: fixed transhumance live in a semi-permanent shelter on high ground such as 32. __________
items.analysis: 答案解析：第32题问固定型迁牧者在高地住的半永久性庇护所例子是什么。原文说 such as a cabin，所以答案是 Cabin。

items.title: nomadic transhumance always stay in portable shelters, e.g. homes of the Gabra people carried on 33. __________
items.analysis: 答案解析：第33题问 Gabra 人的住所由什么动物驮着。原文说 carries the frame of his home on his camel，所以答案是 Camel。

items.title: for fixed transhumance, only 34. __________ pastoralists make the journey
items.analysis: 答案解析：第34题问固定型迁牧中只有哪些牧民会进行迁移。原文说 These are normally the young ones，所以答案是 Young。

items.title: people who do not leave the valleys may become 35. __________
items.analysis: 答案解析：第35题问不离开山谷的人可能会成为谁。原文说 start working as farmers，所以答案是 Farmers。

items.title: exchange animals with other communities for 36. __________
items.analysis: 答案解析：第36题问他们用动物与其他社区交换什么。原文说 trade animals for grain，所以答案是 Grain。

items.title: herds provide all needs—food and 37. __________
items.analysis: 答案解析：第37题问畜群提供所有需求，包括食物和什么。原文说 food and also their clothing from their herds，所以答案是 Clothing。

items.title: they traditionally arrive in time to help local residents with the 38. __________
items.analysis: 答案解析：第38题问他们传统上到达后帮助当地居民做什么。原文说 assisted local farmers with the harvest，所以答案是 Harvest。

items.title: their homes are 39. __________, which are temporary shelters
items.analysis: 答案解析：第39题问他们的住所是什么临时庇护所。原文说 they live in tents，所以答案是 Tents。

items.title: a better term to describe this type of transhumance might be 40. __________
items.analysis: 答案解析：第40题问更适合描述这种迁牧类型的术语是什么。原文说 more precisely refer to as intermediate，所以答案是 Intermediate。

groups.source_collect:
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
Transhumance
Two Types of Transhumance
• fixed: mainly in countries with predictable seasons, e.g. Switzerland
• nomadic: in areas with unpredictable climatic conditions, e.g. Somalia
Study on the Movement of Pastoralists
• for fixed transhumance, the pattern of movement is described as $31$
• while for nomadic transhumance, the pattern is more complex and varied
Differences in terms of Homes
• fixed transhumance live in a semi-permanent shelter on high ground such as $32$
• nomadic transhumance always stay in portable shelters, e.g. homes of the Gabra people carried on $33$
Number of People Involved
• for fixed transhumance, only $34$ pastoralists make the journey
• however, all nomadic transhumance migrate with their livestock
Diet and Goods
• people who do not leave the valleys may become $35$
• exchange animals with other communities for $36$
• herds provide all needs—food and $37$
Research in Algeria in North Africa
• they traditionally arrive in time to help local residents with the $38$
• their homes are $39$, which are temporary shelters
• a better term to describe this type of transhumance might be $40$

source_credit: 此内容由🍠@Listening 永不虾滑整理，仅作为分享供个人使用；禁止商用、倒卖和引流。有问题请先仔细看🍠这个账号的置顶和分享贴。没有团队，不是机构，纯个人分享。督学和其他相关课程请咨询主页另一个号@心情有点虾滑（不回任何机经相关消息）。
